title: Duplicate Ipv6Error types in lichen-ipv6 and lichen-core with incompatible variants
description:
Two different Ipv6Error enums exist:

lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs:
  pub enum Ipv6Error {
      TooShort,  // unit variant
      WrongVersion(u8),
      BufferTooSmall { needed: usize, available: usize },  // inline struct
  }

lichen-core/src/ipv6.rs:
  pub enum Ipv6Error {
      TooShort(TooShort),  // wraps core error type
      WrongVersion(u8),
      InvalidExtensionLength,
      FragmentNotSupported,
      BufferTooSmall(BufferTooSmall),  // wraps core error type
  }

The lichen-core version is richer (more variants, wraps structured errors) while lichen-ipv6 has simpler inline variants.

What goes wrong: Code that handles Ipv6Error cannot work across both crates. lichen-core depends on lichen-ipv6 but defines its own incompatible error type. This suggests one should be consolidated or one should be internal-only.
close_reason: Implemented wrapped TooShort/BufferTooSmall error types using lichen-core::error re-exports from lichen-ipv6; updated lichen-senml, lichen-oscore, and lichen-rpl to use the new wrapped types with proper size information
